intermediate_mechanisms: IL-23 production by dendritic cells drives Th17 polarization
Recurrent abscess cycles with chronic inflammation

edges.description: Neutrophilic abscess formation directly manifests as the recurrent skin abscess phenotype.
Painful abscesses are a major contributor to chronic pain in HS patients.

intermediate_mechanisms: Sustained cytokine milieu activates fibroblast chemokine programs

edges.description: Pathogenic keratinocytes produce IL-1 and complement that amplify innate immune activation.

intermediate_mechanisms: Autoantibodies and complement activation damage tissue
NET formation contributes to tissue destruction

edges.description: Chronic sinus tracts directly manifest as the draining sinus tract phenotype.
Sinus tracts contribute to chronic pain through persistent inflammation and drainage.
Epithelialized tunnels, keratinous detritus, and dilated follicles create protected niches where anaerobic dysbiosis and large bacterial aggregates can persist.

Anaerobic dysbiosis and biofilm-associated bacterial products may provide PAMP input into keratinocyte and myeloid TLR/IL-1R pathways, reinforcing innate inflammation after follicular rupture or within established tunnels.
Recurrent tissue destruction directly produces hypertrophic and atrophic scarring.

nodes.description: Neutrophilic infiltration at the site of follicular rupture leads to abscess formation. Dense collections of neutrophils, cellular debris, and purulent material form painful nodules in intertriginous skin.

evidence.reference_title: Hidradenitis Suppurativa: Advances in Diagnosis and Treatment.
evidence.snippet: The diagnosis of HS is made by lesion morphology (nodules, abscesses, tunnels, and scars), location (axillae, inframammary folds, groin, perigenital, or perineal), and lesion progression (2 recurrences within 6 months or chronic or persistent lesions for ≥3 months).

nodes.description: Secukinumab targets IL-17A-mediated neutrophilic inflammation. SUNSHINE/SUNRISE phase III trials demonstrated sustained efficacy through 52 weeks in moderate-to-severe HS.

evidence.reference_title: Long-term efficacy and safety of secukinumab in patients with moderate-to-severe hidradenitis suppurativa: week 104 results from the SUNSHINE and SUNRISE extension trial.
evidence.snippet: The SUNSHINE and SUNRISE phase III trials demonstrated sustained clinical efficacy of secukinumab in patients with moderate-to-severe hidradenitis suppurativa (HS) through 52 weeks.

Recent advancements in genetic research have identified potential therapeutic targets, leading to the development of anti-TNF-α, anti-IL-17, anti-IL-1α, and anti-IL-12/23 therapies and JAK inhibitors.

nodes.description: Adalimumab is the first-line biologic approved for moderate-to-severe HS. It reduces inflammation by neutralizing TNF-alpha. PIONEER I/II phase III trials showed HiSCR response rates of 42-59% versus 26-28% placebo at week 12.

evidence.reference_title: Two Phase 3 Trials of Adalimumab for Hidradenitis Suppurativa.
evidence.snippet: Clinical response rates at week 12 were significantly higher for the groups receiving adalimumab weekly than for the placebo groups: 41.8% versus 26.0% in PIONEER I (P=0.003) and 58.9% versus 27.6% in PIONEER II (P<0.001).

evidence.reference_title: Skin immune-mesenchymal interplay within tertiary lymphoid structures promotes autoimmune pathogenesis in hidradenitis suppurativa.
evidence.snippet: early TNF-α blockade suppressed aggregate initiation.

nodes.description: Significant chronic pain from active lesions and sinus tracts, major contributor to reduced quality of life.

evidence.reference_title: Acne inversa (Hidradenitis suppurativa): A review with a focus on pathogenesis and treatment.
evidence.snippet: Acne inversa (AI) is a disabilitating chronic inflammatory disease with major negative impact on quality of life and significant co-morbidities.

nodes.description: Epithelialized tunnels connecting abscesses beneath the skin, characteristic of moderate to severe disease.

evidence.reference_title: Skin immune-mesenchymal interplay within tertiary lymphoid structures promotes autoimmune pathogenesis in hidradenitis suppurativa.
evidence.snippet: Hidradenitis suppurativa (HS) is a chronic, debilitating inflammatory skin disease characterized by keratinized epithelial tunnels that grow deeply into the dermis.

nodes.description: Bimekizumab selectively inhibits both IL-17A and IL-17F. BE HEARD I and II phase III trials demonstrated HiSCR50 response rates of 48-52% versus 29-32% placebo at week 16, with responses maintained or increased to week 48.

evidence.reference_title: Efficacy and safety of bimekizumab in patients with moderate-to-severe hidradenitis suppurativa (BE HEARD I and BE HEARD II): two 48-week, randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, multicentre phase 3 trials.
evidence.snippet: Bimekizumab was well tolerated by patients with hidradenitis suppurativa and produced rapid and deep clinically meaningful responses that were maintained up to 48 weeks.

nodes.description: HS basal progenitor keratinocytes undergo epigenetic rewiring with extensive chromatin remodeling (1,434 gained / 3,722 lost ATAC peaks) that primes inflammatory gene programs. This produces pathogenic keratinocyte clones marked by S100A7/8/9 and KRT6 that trigger IL-1, IL-10, and complement cascades. This represents an epithelial-intrinsic disease driver.

evidence.reference_title: Epigenetic switch reshapes epithelial progenitor cell signatures and drives inflammatory pathogenesis in hidradenitis suppurativa.
evidence.snippet: we investigated HS epithelial cells and demonstrated that HS basal progenitors modulate their lineage restriction and give rise to pathogenic keratinocyte clones, resulting in epidermal hyperproliferation and dysregulated inflammation in HS.

evidence.reference_title: Epigenetic switch reshapes epithelial progenitor cell signatures and drives inflammatory pathogenesis in hidradenitis suppurativa.
evidence.snippet: we identified a keratinocyte population specific to HS. This population is marked by S100A7/8/9 and KRT6 family members, triggering IL1, IL10, and complement inflammatory cascades.

nodes.description: Aberrant keratinization of the hair follicle infundibulum leads to follicular plugging. Hyperproliferative keratinocytes produce excess keratin that occludes the follicular ostium. In the canonical occlusion-first model this is the initiating event in HS pathogenesis; in the emerging autoinflammatory keratinization model it may be coupled to epithelial-intrinsic immune activation rather than purely preceding it.

evidence.reference_title: Hidradenitis suppurativa is an autoinflammatory keratinization disease: A review of the clinical, histologic, and molecular evidence.
evidence.snippet: The pathogenic model of hidradenitis suppurativa is in the midst of a paradigm shift away from a disorder of primary follicular occlusion to an autoinflammatory keratinization disease.

evidence.reference_title: Gamma-secretase gene mutations in familial acne inversa.
evidence.snippet: Acne inversa (AI), also known as hidradenitis suppurativa, is a chronic, recurrent, inflammatory disease of hair follicles that often runs in families.

evidence.reference_title: Hidradenitis suppurativa: Epidemiology, clinical presentation, and pathogenesis.
evidence.snippet: Although the mechanism of HS has not been entirely elucidated, lesion formation is believed to center around follicular hyperkeratosis within the pilosebaceous-apocrine unit.

nodes.description: Continued keratinization causes follicular dilation and eventual mechanical rupture, releasing keratin, commensal bacteria, and damage-associated molecular patterns (DAMPs) into the surrounding dermis. This triggers the innate immune response.

evidence.reference_title: Hidradenitis suppurativa is an autoinflammatory keratinization disease: A review of the clinical, histologic, and molecular evidence.
evidence.snippet: The process of follicular rupture and dermal tunnel formation can be explained as secondary responses to inflammatory activation of fibroblasts and epithelial-mesenchymal transition, with antibody production associated with inflammatory amplification in advanced disease.

nodes.description: Anti-androgen therapy (e.g., spironolactone, cyproterone acetate) may benefit some female patients by modulating hormonal triggers contributing to follicular occlusion.

evidence.snippet: The disease's association with a predisposing genetic background, obesity, smoking, and skin occlusion underscores the complexity of its etiology.

meta.role: Adjunctive for female patients with hormonal flares

nodes.description: KT-474 (SAR444656) is a novel IRAK4 degrader that targets TLR/IL-1R-driven inflammation. Phase 1 trial demonstrated IRAK4 degradation of ≥95% in blood with normalization in skin and 48-63% reduction of inflammatory biomarkers.

evidence.reference_title: IRAK4 degrader in hidradenitis suppurativa and atopic dermatitis: a phase 1 trial.
evidence.snippet: Reduction of disease-relevant inflammatory biomarkers was demonstrated in the blood and skin of patients with HS and patients with AD and was associated with improvement in skin lesions and symptoms.

nodes.description: Following follicular rupture, DAMPs and PAMPs activate TLR2 and inflammasome pathways (NLRP3/CASP1). This drives IL-1beta and IL-36 release from keratinocytes and myeloid cells, recruiting neutrophils and macrophages to form the early inflammatory infiltrate and abscess.

evidence.reference_title: Evidence on Hidradenitis Suppurativa as an Autoinflammatory Skin Disease.
evidence.snippet: In the early phase of the disease, the innate immune system is hyperactivated, contributing to tissue damage and triggering the activation and amplification of the adaptive immune response, which plays a pivotal role in the chronic stages of the disease.

evidence.reference_title: IRAK4 degrader in hidradenitis suppurativa and atopic dermatitis: a phase 1 trial.
evidence.snippet: Toll-like receptor-driven and interleukin-1 (IL-1) receptor-driven inflammation mediated by IL-1 receptor-associated kinase 4 (IRAK4) is involved in the pathophysiology of hidradenitis suppurativa (HS) and atopic dermatitis (AD).

nodes.description: HS lesions and tunnels harbor distinct polymicrobial, predominantly anaerobic communities with reduced aerobic commensals. Biofilm-associated bacterial aggregates are common in chronic lesions and tunnels and may amplify TLR/IL-1R innate inflammation, but current evidence does not resolve whether dysbiosis is a primary trigger, an inflammatory amplifier, or a consequence of follicular rupture and tunnel anatomy.

evidence.reference_title: The Microbiological Landscape of Anaerobic Infections in Hidradenitis Suppurativa: A Prospective Metagenomic Study.
evidence.snippet: High-throughput sequencing identified 43 taxa associated with HS lesions.

evidence.reference_title: The Microbiome in Hidradenitis Suppurativa Tunnels: A Systematic Review.
evidence.snippet: The findings from these studies suggested that HS tunnels are characterized by a polymicrobial microbiome, predominantly dominated by anaerobic bacteria such as Porphyromonas, Parvimonas, Fusobacterium, and Prevotella.

evidence.reference_title: The Microbiome in Hidradenitis Suppurativa: A Review.
evidence.snippet: Coagulase-negative Staphylococcus, anaerobes such as Porphyromonas and Prevotella, and Staphylococcus aureus species were commonly encountered organisms across the included cutaneous microbiome studies.

evidence.reference_title: Bacterial biofilm in chronic lesions of hidradenitis suppurativa.
evidence.snippet: This study suggests that biofilm formation is associated with inflammation of chronic HS lesions.

nodes.description: Painful, deep-seated nodules and abscesses primarily in intertriginous areas including axillae, groin, and perianal regions.

evidence.reference_title: Hidradenitis Suppurativa: Advances in Diagnosis and Treatment.
evidence.snippet: The diagnosis of HS is made by lesion morphology (nodules, abscesses, tunnels, and scars), location (axillae, inframammary folds, groin, perigenital, or perineal), and lesion progression (2 recurrences within 6 months or chronic or persistent lesions for ≥3 months).

nodes.description: Hypertrophic and atrophic scarring resulting from chronic inflammation and tissue destruction.

evidence.reference_title: Acne inversa (Hidradenitis suppurativa): A review with a focus on pathogenesis and treatment.
evidence.snippet: Acne inversa (AI) is a disabilitating chronic inflammatory disease with major negative impact on quality of life and significant co-morbidities.

nodes.description: Chronic inflammation and recurrent abscess cycles lead to tissue destruction and formation of epithelialized sinus tracts (tunnels) that grow deeply into the dermis. These tracts serve as persistent inflammatory niches and sites of TLS formation, perpetuating the disease cycle.

evidence.reference_title: Skin immune-mesenchymal interplay within tertiary lymphoid structures promotes autoimmune pathogenesis in hidradenitis suppurativa.
evidence.snippet: Hidradenitis suppurativa (HS) is a chronic, debilitating inflammatory skin disease characterized by keratinized epithelial tunnels that grow deeply into the dermis.

nodes.description: Wide excision of affected tissue for Hurley Stage II-III disease with extensive sinus tracts. Deroofing and CO2 laser excision are alternatives for localized disease.

evidence.reference_title: Acne inversa (Hidradenitis suppurativa): A review with a focus on pathogenesis and treatment.
evidence.snippet: The standard of therapy in advanced cases is surgery with wide excisions and healing by secondary intention. This treatment results in significant reduction of complaints and achieves satisfactory body contouring.

meta.role: Definitive treatment for advanced disease with sinus tracts

nodes.description: In chronic HS lesions, organized tertiary lymphoid structures (TLSs) form near epithelialized tunnels. These TLSs contain proliferative T cells (including Tfh, Treg, and IL-17A+/IFNG+ pathogenic T cells) and clonally expanded plasma cells producing anti-keratinocyte autoantibodies. HS fibroblasts expressing CXCL13 or CCL19 orchestrate lymphocyte aggregation via TNF-alpha feedback loops.

evidence.reference_title: Skin immune-mesenchymal interplay within tertiary lymphoid structures promotes autoimmune pathogenesis in hidradenitis suppurativa.
evidence.snippet: Multi-omics profiling and multiplexed imaging identified tertiary lymphoid structures (TLSs) near HS tunnels. These TLSs were enriched with proliferative T cells, including follicular helper (Tfh), regulatory (Treg), and pathogenic T cells (IL17A+ and IFNG+), alongside extensive clonal expansion of plasma cells producing antibodies reactive to keratinocytes.

evidence.reference_title: Skin immune-mesenchymal interplay within tertiary lymphoid structures promotes autoimmune pathogenesis in hidradenitis suppurativa.
evidence.snippet: HS fibroblasts express CXCL13 or CCL19 in response to immune cytokines. Using a microfluidic system to mimic TLS on a chip, we found that HS fibroblasts critically orchestrated lymphocyte aggregation via tumor necrosis factor alpha (TNF-α)-CXCL13 and TNF-α-CCL19 feedback loops with B and T cells, respectively; early TNF-α blockade suppressed aggregate initiation.

nodes.description: IL-23 from dendritic cells drives Th17 polarization. IL-17A/F-producing T cells amplify neutrophil recruitment via CXCL1/8 and sustain chronic inflammation. The Th17:Treg imbalance perpetuates the inflammatory cycle.

evidence.snippet: The dysregulation of immune mediators, including TNF-α, IL-17, IL-1β, and IL-12/23, plays a crucial role in the chronic inflammatory nature of HS.

evidence.reference_title: Skin immune-mesenchymal interplay within tertiary lymphoid structures promotes autoimmune pathogenesis in hidradenitis suppurativa.
evidence.snippet: These TLSs were enriched with proliferative T cells, including follicular helper (Tfh), regulatory (Treg), and pathogenic T cells (IL17A+ and IFNG+), alongside extensive clonal expansion of plasma cells producing antibodies reactive to keratinocytes.

nodes.description: Recurrent cycles of inflammation, abscess formation, and tissue remodeling result in progressive tissue destruction. Fibroblast activation and extracellular matrix remodeling produce hypertrophic and atrophic scarring.

evidence.reference_title: Acne inversa (Hidradenitis suppurativa): A review with a focus on pathogenesis and treatment.
evidence.snippet: Acne inversa (AI) is a disabilitating chronic inflammatory disease with major negative impact on quality of life and significant co-morbidities.
